mnemonics.title: mkdir Parameters
mnemonics.content: MKDIR = Make Directory: 'mkdir(path, mode)' - path first, then permissions like a coat (mode)

mnemonics.title: rmdir Rules
mnemonics.content: RMDIR = Remove Directory: Must be EMPTY, NOT current, MUST exist

acronyms.usage: Creates a new directory in the filesystem
Removes an empty directory from the filesystem
Variable that holds error codes from failed system calls
Error returned when trying to create something that already exists
Error returned when trying to remove a non-empty directory

visualTips.visualization: Think of mkdir() as creating a new folder in a filing cabinet. The mode determines who can access it (owner, group, others).
Think of rmdir() as removing an empty folder. You can only remove it if there's nothing inside and you're not standing in it.
Directories form a tree. mkdir() adds a new branch, rmdir() prunes an empty leaf.

analogies.analogy: Like building a new room in a house. You need to specify who can enter (permissions mode) and the room must have proper foundation (parent directory must exist).
Like removing an empty box from storage. You can only remove it if it's completely empty - you can't remove a box that still has items inside.
Similar to hotel room access cards: owner has full access (rwx), group has limited access (r-x), others have minimal access (--x).
